description_long: This is a weathered, industrial-style traffic light mounted on a cylindrical metal pole. The pole supports a vertical stack of three circular signal lights, each housed in a rusted, boxy enclosure. Above the lights, there is a diamond-shaped metal sign plate, also showing signs of rust and age. The pole features a midsection collar and a rectangular box-like structure, possibly for mounting or housing electrical components. The entire object has a consistent rusty brown coloration, suggesting prolonged outdoor exposure. The design is utilitarian, with no visible pedestrian signals or decorative elements.
description: A rusted, pole-mounted traffic light with three vertically stacked signal lights and a diamond-shaped sign on top. The structure is industrial and weathered, with a brownish, corroded metal finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing the three circular signal lights stacked vertically and the diamond-shaped sign above.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the slim profile of the pole and the protruding signal light housings.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the pole, the diamond sign, and the mounting box, with no visible lights.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slim profile and the side of the signal light housings.
